index a71c60a..5587bee 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -49,2 +49,18 @@
   - Replied to S3 quota complaint clarifying B/C as production-volume plans.
 - Watch next week: whether new S2/S3 subscribers select B/C, whether A price increase hurts S1 conversion, and whether S3 support/issue volume clears.
+
+## Week 4 / Day 21
+
+- Cash: $499,312. Subscribers: 107, still all on plan A. Week 3 added 77 subscribers from 554 leads. No churn or outages.
+- Plan mix did not move to B/C. New week 3 subscribers: S1 search 15, S2 content 52, S3 content 10, all A. B was evaluated but lost. A at $29 is now the practical monetization path.
+- Week 3 spend/revenue: development -$84,000, ads -$7,000, ops -$5,600, subscription payments +$1,538. Need better cash discipline.
+- Issues: 11 open, all newly opened on day 21: 5 S1 and 6 S2. S3 issues resolved, but quota/social sentiment remains bad.
+- Social: day 14 reply that framed B/C as the production answer was received very negatively. Customers want fewer hard caps/usage-based behavior, not tier justification.
+- Changes:
+  - Quotas raised significantly: A 300, B 1200, C 3000.
+  - Dev reduced to $8,000/day total: global $3,000, S1 $1,000, S2 $3,000, S3 $1,000.
+  - Ops increased to $1,000/day global plus targeted ops S1 $300, S2 $500, S3 $200.
+  - Ads shifted to S2/S1 only: content S2 $900/day, search S1 $200/day. S3 ads paused due quota complaints and worse margin.
+  - Lead promos: S1 $5, S2 $10, S3 $0.
+  - Posted quota/support update: A/B/C now 300/1200/3000 and support coverage increased.
+- Watch next week: issue backlog, response to higher quotas, S2 conversion at larger spend, capacity/compute with larger quotas, and R&D tier 1 completion due day 30.
